danger_signals.visual_form: Crab-like creatures, scorpions, multi-headed entities with distinct alien morphology
danger_signals.meaning: UFO invasion threat; non-human hostile entities occupying city spaces

danger_signals.visual_form: Enormous alien boss characters with animated limbs, projectile weapons, eye tracking mechanics
danger_signals.meaning: Major alien command structure; escalating threat requiring puzzle piece collection to progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Final spaceship arena with turrets and computer systems
danger_signals.meaning: Source of invasion; ultimate danger location requiring final confrontation

danger_signals.visual_form: Bullets from enemies and boss turrets; wall-collision physics (NA version) vs. wall-piercing (EU version)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; difficulty variance between regions communicated through projectile behavior

danger_signals.visual_form: Constant enemy spawn rates in streets and sewers
danger_signals.meaning: Persistent invasion; ongoing threat requiring constant vigilance

safety_signals.visual_form: Seven houses scattered throughout city (Thing's house ×3, Wednesday's, Gomez's, Morticia's, Grandmama's, Pugsley's) plus Addams mansion via secret tree path
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones where family members provide weapons and items; narrative progression checkpoints

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapons (gun variants), special items (Noose to summon Lurch), puzzle pieces from defeated bosses
safety_signals.meaning: Power progression; capability enhancement for survival

safety_signals.visual_form: Protective enchantment on Addams Family at game start
safety_signals.meaning: Family safety established; narrative protection enabling player to proceed without family jeopardy

safety_signals.visual_form: Pieces granted upon boss defeat; collected progressively throughout game
safety_signals.meaning: Story progression; cleared threat zones; advancement toward final confrontation

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Addams mansion; scattered family homes throughout city
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Seven family houses + secret mansion access; game locations tied to individual family members

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: UFO; alien morphology (crabs, scorpions, multi-headed creatures, computer systems)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Crab-like aliens spread bubbles when damaged; scorpions fire projectiles; four-headed directional-fire entities; giant bosses with articulated limbs and turret systems
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sewers and UFO platform as primary alien-occupied zones; alien presence in city streets
City streets; referenced in narrative as kidnapped by UFO beams

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: City with streets, buildings, sewers accessible; normal urban environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs (Addams family members) stationed in houses; ready to provide aid

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien enemies throughout streets and sewers; UFO visible as final arena; enemy respawning continuous
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Townspeople beamed up; family members protected by Grandmama's spell; Fester must combat alien presence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Building becomes inaccessible after boss defeat; puzzle piece and UFO image displayed; player items refilled
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Fester cannot backtrack through cleared building; forced forward progression; threat from that zone eliminated

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UFO platform with computer core boss; turrets with charging mechanics; eye tracking animation (unused feature suggests intended dynamic behavior)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ultimate confrontation; all five bosses defeated prerequisite; final threat must be neutralized

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: UFO destruction sequence with lightning overlay and ship explosion graphics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Invasion averted; townspeople saved; Addams family protection maintained

environmental_storytelling.example: Three overhead areas (streets, sewers, UFO platform) + six interior buildings with 3D perspective create layered invasion geography; sewers as access routes to otherwise blocked areas signal underground resistance pathways

Seven scattered Addams family homes + secret mansion access through trees establish family as distributed throughout city; each location provides specific weapons/items tying character personality to tool provision
Five buildings each housing one alien boss establishes vertical threat hierarchy; final UFO boss as ultimate enemy; unused two-boss final area graphics suggest originally planned more complex endgame
NA version bullets collide with walls (harder difficulty); EU version bullets pierce walls (easier difficulty) — same invasion scenario communicated at different threat intensities based on region

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: All items restored after each boss victory signals reset/reinforcement; puzzle piece collection tracks invasion progress toward final confrontation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spreads three bubbles; slowly approaches Fester; behavior mirrors larger aliens in spaceship area
Fires projectile from tail; designed for ground-level threat
Fires in four directions; horizontal sprite mirrored and overlaid with vertical head sets for omnidirectional threat
Fires projectiles from hands; walks; launches jaw with 90-degree rotation capability; multi-phase threat design
Eye static in-game but animated tiles suggest intended eye-tracking following Fester; turrets fire lasers; unused charging particle graphics indicate intended visual progression
Destroys all enemies on screen; emergency defensive capability tied to special item
Provide weapons and items; do not engage combat; serve as support nodes rather than active combatants

notes: Fester's Quest manifests invasion through distributed enemy presence across three overhead zones and building interiors. World-state communication relies heavily on spatial progression (buildings become inaccessible after boss defeat, forcing narrative advancement) and item/puzzle piece collection as visible victory markers. Faction distinction is minimal — clear alien vs. family binary with no political complexity. Regional difficulty variants (EU vs. NA) alter the same invasive scenario's perceived threat through projectile physics, suggesting designers understood visual/mechanical communication of danger intensity. Unused graphics (two potential final bosses, animated turret angles, eye-tracking mechanics, explosive animation) indicate originally planned more complex manifestation system for final confrontation. The game uses character-location anchoring (each family member in distinct house) to reinforce narrative identity within mechanical progression.
